cases.title: Scenario-matrix coverage for lecturer opening scheduled session
preconditions: Lecturer user is authenticated and assigned to the target class section.
A class session exists in Scheduled state for that section.
steps: Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open with lecturer token and valid Idempotency-Key.
Read the open response payload and metadata.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current immediately after opening.
cases.expected: Open call succeeds and maps to testing-plan session open/close row: session is Open, openedAt and actor context are recorded, and QR endpoint returns Valid token metadata with expiry.

cases.title: Session control module writes open and close state transitions with DB side effects
preconditions: Database contains a Scheduled session and enrolled students for an assigned lecturer section.
Integration test uses real transaction boundaries for session and attendance tables.
steps: Invoke session open use-case (or POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open path through API service) as Lecturer.
Assert persisted state changes include state Open and non-null openedAt/openedBy fields.
Invoke close flow as same Lecturer via POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close.
Assert persisted state Closed, closedAt/closedBy fields, and unresolved students finalized per close policy.
cases.expected: Module + repository integration commits legal Scheduled->Open->Closed transitions and required persistence side effects in one consistent DB-backed flow.

cases.title: Only valid pre-state Scheduled can transition to Open
preconditions: Session fixtures exist in Scheduled, Open, and Closed states.
steps: Attempt open transition for Scheduled session as assigned Lecturer.
Attempt open transition for already Open session.
Attempt open transition for Closed session.
cases.expected: Scheduled session transitions to Open; Open/Closed pre-states are rejected with invalid-transition behavior and no illegal state persistence.

cases.title: Concurrent duplicate check-in submissions keep one success record per student-session
preconditions: Session is Open and student is enrolled.
Test harness can fire parallel check-in commands with same actor and token.
steps: Submit two or more POST /v1/check-ins requests in parallel for the same student and session.
Collect API outcomes and query attendance/check-in-attempt persistence.
cases.expected: Exactly one successful attendance result exists for the student-session; additional concurrent submissions are rejected as DuplicateCheckIn and attempts are logged with structured outcomes.

cases.title: HTTP contract for opening session returns expected status and envelope
steps: Send POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open with Authorization and Idempotency-Key headers.
Validate HTTP status code and JSON envelope shape.
Validate data fields state, openedAt, and qr.expiresAt are present and consistent.
cases.expected: Response is 200 with standard envelope {data, meta, error}; data.state is Open and includes QR metadata required by API contract.

cases.title: Unauthorized roles are denied scoped report and export access
preconditions: Student and out-of-scope Lecturer accounts exist.
Attendance data exists outside each actor's authorized scope.
steps: Call GET /v1/reports/attendance as Student and as out-of-scope Lecturer.
Call POST /v1/exports/attendance with filters outside actor scope for same accounts.
cases.expected: Requests are denied with authorization errors (Forbidden/OutOfScope semantics), and responses do not leak restricted rows, counts, or export artifacts.

cases.title: Check-in after close boundary returns SessionClosed and no new success
preconditions: Session was opened and then closed by assigned Lecturer.
Student is enrolled and has not yet successfully checked in.
steps: Submit POST /v1/check-ins using token linked to the now-closed session.
Read response code and error.code.
Query attendance record for the student-session pair.
cases.expected: API rejects post-close attempt with SessionClosed semantics and no successful attendance row is created.

cases.title: Lecturer browser journey opens session and sees live open-state indicators
preconditions: Lecturer is logged in on desktop browser and assigned to section.
Session appears in Scheduled state on lecturer session management page.
steps: Open session list page and select a Scheduled session row.
Trigger Open Attendance action from UI.
Observe session badge/state, opened time indicator, and QR panel refresh behavior without full page reload.
cases.expected: UI transitions session row from Scheduled to Open, shows openedAt metadata and active QR display/refresh indicators consistent with backend open state.
